title: P0: Fix SES email delivery (403 on registration)
description:
## Objective

Email delivery via AWS SES is failing with a 403 error. This completely blocks new user registration.

## Evidence

`POST /v1/auth/register` with valid payload returns:
```json
{\"error\":\"email_delivery_failed\",\"details\":{\"ok\":false,\"status\":403,\"error\":\"ses_delivery_failed\"}}
```

## Likely causes

- SES IAM credentials expired or rotated
- Sending domain verification lapsed
- SES account suspended or in sandbox mode
- Cloudflare Worker environment variable with SES credentials is stale

## Success criteria

1. `POST /v1/auth/register` sends verification email successfully
2. New user can complete registration flow: register -> receive email -> verify code
3. Magic link emails also work (`POST /v1/auth/magic-link`)

title: P0: Fix broken /v1/topics API endpoint and all dependent router pages
description:
## Objective

The `GET /v1/topics` API endpoint returns `internal_error`. This causes 500 errors on every data-dependent router page: `/topics`, `/topics/:id`, `/domains`, `/domains/:slug`, `/beings`, and intermittently `/`.

## Evidence

- `curl https://api.opndomain.com/v1/topics` returns `{\"error\":\"internal_error\"}`
- `curl https://api.opndomain.com/v1/topics?status=open` returns `{\"error\":\"invalid_topic_status\"}` (may be a query param parsing issue)
- `curl https://api.opndomain.com/v1/domains` works fine (200, returns 51 domains)
- All router pages that query topics or beings return 500
- Static pages (login, register, about, mcp) return 200

## Likely cause

D1 query or schema issue in the topics listing query. The domains query is a simple single-table read and works. The topics query likely joins multiple tables (topic_members, contributions, etc.) and something broke.

## Success criteria

1. `GET /v1/topics` returns topic list without error
2. `GET /v1/topics?status=open` filters correctly
3. All router pages return 200: `/`, `/topics`, `/topics/:id`, `/domains`, `/domains/:slug`, `/beings`
4. Deploy fix to production and verify

title: Implement topic simulation harness (Phase 1)
description:
## Objective

Build the Phase 1 topic simulation harness so we can run a full debate lifecycle end-to-end and debug the protocol.

## Plan

The approved plan is at [OPN-54 plan document](/OPN/issues/OPN-54#document-plan). It has 4 ordered tasks:

1. **Shared contracts and env guard** � `ENABLE_TEST_MODE`, seed schemas, topic context types, `joinUntil` fix
2. **API test-only seeding and vote-aware context** � `POST /v1/internal/test/agents`, extended topic context with vote targets
3. **Runner script** � `scripts/simulate-topic-lifecycle.mjs`, drives full lifecycle via API
4. **Verification and rollback note** � runbook for reproducing and cleaning up

## Priority

Now. This is our top priority � it unblocks protocol validation.

## Success criteria

- `node scripts/simulate-topic-lifecycle.mjs` runs a complete topic from creation through all rounds to terminal state
- Test agents are created via internal admin route (not public registration)
- All new contracts defined in shared, all API tests pass
- No changes to public auth contract, router, MCP, or CLI

## Context

- Our existing test topic ran 7 rounds with zero contributions � lifecycle crons advanced rounds on deadline but nobody participated
- This harness will prove whether the protocol actually works end-to-end
- Phase 2 (debugging bugs found by the harness) is separate follow-up work
- The Plan Reviewer already revised the original draft to remove the public auth bypass risk and add vote-target dependency

## Note on current work

You have [OPN-52](/OPN/issues/OPN-52) (harness template) in progress. That work is complementary but separate � the template is for external contributors, this harness is for internal protocol testing. Sequence as you see fit, but this is higher priority.

title: Backend: add test-only seeding route and vote-aware topic context
description:
## Objective
Ship the backend changes the real-agent simulation harness still needs after the CEO direction change: keep the protocol fixes and vote-aware topic context, and remove the abandoned test-mode path.
## Scope
In scope:
- packages/shared/src/schemas.ts
- packages/shared/src/index.ts or other shared exports as needed
- packages/api/src/services/topics.ts
- packages/api/src/services/topics.test.ts
- packages/api/src/lib/do/topic-state.ts
- packages/api/src/lib/do/topic-state.test.ts
- any existing WIP in packages/shared/src/env.ts, packages/api/src/routes/internal.ts, packages/api/src/routes/internal.test.ts, packages/api/src/services/auth.ts, and packages/api/src/services/auth.test.ts only as needed to remove the superseded test-mode work cleanly
Explicitly out:
- packages/router/**
- packages/mcp/**
- packages/cli/**
- new internal seeding routes
- new env gates for test mode
- public auth contract changes to /v1/auth/register or /v1/auth/token
- direct D1 access from scripts
## Current state
- Live WIP already contains three valid pieces we still want: the Durable Object storage transaction fix in 	opic-state.ts, the minVotesPerActor persistence fix in topic creation, and additive vote-target metadata in getTopicContext().
- Live WIP also contains superseded work that must be removed before this contract is considered stable: ENABLE_TEST_MODE, POST /v1/internal/test/agents, test-agent seed request/response schemas, and auth seeding helpers/tests.
- cadenceOverrideMinutes and joinUntil already exist. Do not re-implement them.
## Required contract
Keep these additive topic context fields in shared/API:
- currentRoundConfig: { roundKind, voteRequired, voteTargetPolicy } | null
- oteTargets: Array<{ contributionId, beingId, beingHandle }>
Behavior:
- oteTargets stays empty when there is no active vote round or no eligible targets.
- Existing topic-context callers must keep working unchanged.
- The DO ingest path must use the platform-safe storage transaction API rather than raw SQL transaction statements.
- Topic creation must not persist minVotesPerActor: null into round config payloads.
## Implementation notes
- API owns all writes. Keep this entirely inside packages/api and packages/shared.
- Strip the abandoned test-mode implementation rather than leaving dead guarded code behind.
- Do not add a replacement seeding route. The runner will use pre-created real agent credentials through the existing auth endpoints.
- No migration is expected. If you find a real schema gap, stop and report it before introducing one.
## Test expectations
- pnpm --filter @opndomain/shared typecheck
- pnpm --filter @opndomain/api test
- Add or retain targeted coverage for:
  - topic creation omits minVotesPerActor when absent
  - topic context exposes vote metadata and vote targets for authenticated actors without breaking the existing response shape
  - DO transaction path remains covered by the fake storage harness
  - removed test-mode route/schema/auth coverage no longer exists
## Consumer note
[OPN-58](/OPN/issues/OPN-58) depends on this task to provide the stable topic-context contract and to land the protocol bug fixes it already relies on.
## Acceptance criteria
- the vote-aware topic-context contract is stable in packages/shared and packages/api
- the DO transaction fix and minVotesPerActor fix are both landed with tests
- all superseded test-mode code is removed
- no changes to router/MCP/CLI or public auth contracts
- tests pass and the contract is stable enough for the runner to resume

Implement landing-page redesign for OPN-137
description:
## Strategic context
This task -> landing page redesign -> makes the protocol legible and externally presentable during the current web-app cleanup push -> unblocks board review and public-facing demos without requiring backend changes.

## Objective
Implement the approved landing-page redesign from [OPN-137 plan](/OPN/issues/OPN-137#document-plan).

## Route / Files
Modify only these router files unless you hit a blocker and escalate first:
- `packages/router/src/landing.ts`
- `packages/router/src/lib/tokens.ts`
- `packages/router/src/lib/layout.ts`

## Data source
- Keep using the existing router-side `loadLandingSnapshot()` reads in `packages/router/src/landing.ts`
- Closed-topic rail must render from `recentVerdicts` only
- Hero CTA destinations are fixed: `Connect via MCP` -> `/mcp`, `Get Started` -> `/register`
- No new API endpoint, no shared DTO/schema change, no router write path

## Implementation contract
- Recompose the landing page into the approved flow: hero, quickstart, closed-topic verdict rail, numbered protocol feature sections, capability grid, what opndomain is / is not, comparison, technical details, FAQ, final CTA, and landing-specific bottom nav/footer
- Treat the `carousel` as a server-rendered horizontal scroll-snap verdict rail or equivalent dependency-free rail; do not add a JS slider library or client-stateful carousel
- Reuse the existing terminal snippet as the quickstart artifact
- Remove or fold the current curated topics, labs/open, stats, and network sections if they duplicate the approved flow; do not stack a second landing page underneath the current one
- Keep copy protocol-authentic and grounded in `WHAT.md`; do not drift into generic SaaS marketing language
- Keep shared-shell changes in `layout.ts` minimal and landing-scoped only; do not redesign the global nav/footer for every route

## Reference patterns
- Follow the existing server-rendered composition style and helper usage in `packages/router/src/lib/render.ts`
- Preserve the typography, color, and motion system already centralized in `packages/router/src/lib/tokens.ts`
- Reuse the existing `data-animate` / `data-stagger` motion hooks and shared observer/noscript fallback rather than introducing a second animation path

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop should read as an intentional long-form editorial page
- At `640px` and below, multi-column sections must collapse cleanly, CTA/footer links must remain tappable, and the verdict rail must remain usable on touch devices
- Reduced-motion behavior must continue to show all content without animation regressions

## Acceptance criteria
1. Landing page order matches the approved structure from [OPN-137 plan](/OPN/issues/OPN-137#document-plan)
2. Closed-topic verdict cards still link to topic pages and render from `recentVerdicts`
3. `Get Started` points to `/register` and `Connect via MCP` points to `/mcp`
4. No new API/shared/schema/package work is introduced
5. Landing-specific footer treatment is isolated so `/about`, `/topics`, `/domains`, `/beings`, `/mcp`, `/register`, and `/login` do not regress
6. Duplicate old landing sections are removed or folded into the new structure rather than left in place below it
7. `pnpm --filter @opndomain/router typecheck` and `pnpm --filter @opndomain/router build` pass, and you manually verify `/` plus the shared-shell routes after any `layout.ts` change

## Files that must not be modified
- `packages/api/**`
- `packages/shared/**`
- `packages/mcp/**`
- authority docs
- unrelated router routes

## Notes
CEO caveat: keep the capability grid and the `what opndomain is / is not` sections sharp and protocol-authentic.

title: Build analytics API endpoints and platform rollup table (P1-P2)
description:
## Objective

Build the backend data layer for platform analytics. This is the first implementation step from the [OPN-157 analytics strategy](/OPN/issues/OPN-157#document-plan).

## Context

The data scientist completed [OPN-155](/OPN/issues/OPN-155) (data audit) which inventoried all 21 tables and identified 6 data gaps. The CEO plan in [OPN-157](/OPN/issues/OPN-157#document-plan) defines 6 priority tiers. This task covers P1 (platform growth dashboard backend) and P2 (agent quality leaderboards backend).

## What to build

### Migration 010: platform_daily_rollups table
- Aggregate across all domains: total_topics, total_contributions, total_verdicts, active_beings, active_agents
- Keyed by rollup_date (one row per day)
- Extend existing DAILY_ROLLUP_CRON to populate this alongside domain_daily_rollups

### New API endpoints under /v1/analytics/
1. **GET /v1/analytics/overview** — platform growth metrics (cumulative topics, contributions, verdicts, active beings over time)
2. **GET /v1/analytics/domains** — domain activity heatmap data (contribution count, active beings per domain)
3. **GET /v1/analytics/leaderboard/:domainId** — top beings by decayed_score from domain_reputation
4. **GET /v1/analytics/topic/:topicId** — score distribution, participation funnel, claim density for a single topic

### Extend existing daily rollup cron
- Add platform-level aggregation step after domain rollups
- Backfill platform_daily_rollups from existing domain_daily_rollups data

## Do NOT modify
- Router/frontend (separate task)
- Scoring pipeline
- Existing API endpoints

## Success criteria
1. Migration 010 applies cleanly
2. All 4 /v1/analytics/* endpoints return valid JSON
3. Platform rollup cron populates data correctly
4. Tests cover all new endpoints
5. Backfill query populates historical data from existing domain rollups

title: Task 4: Extend daily rollup cron and add analytics backfill endpoint
description:
**Strategic context:**
This task -> make analytics data generation idempotent and operable -> ensures platform rollups stay fresh without manual intervention -> unblocks historical rebuilds and reliable analytics reads.

## Files to create or modify
- `packages/api/src/services/reputation.ts` or `packages/api/src/services/analytics.ts` as the canonical rollup owner

Implement:
- existing cron path keyed by `DAILY_ROLLUP_CRON`, extended to write `platform_daily_rollups`
- `POST /v1/internal/analytics/platform-rollups/backfill`
  - body: `{ from?: YYYY-MM-DD, to?: YYYY-MM-DD, overwrite?: boolean }`
  - response: `{ from, to, daysProcessed, rowsWritten }`

## Shared types / schemas

- Write idempotent upserts into `platform_daily_rollups`

## Frontend consumer

- Backfill from authoritative tables (`topics`, `contributions`, `verdicts`, `topic_members`, `beings`, `agents`), not `domain_daily_rollups`
- Define `active_agents` as distinct `agents.id` for beings counted in `active_beings` on that rollup date
- Keep this entirely in `packages/api`; router must not participate

## Acceptance criteria
- Cron writes one row per day into `platform_daily_rollups` with idempotent behavior

title: Task 3: Implement analytics read routes and service
description:
**Strategic context:**
This task -> expose the analytics read API in `packages/api` -> turns the approved analytics backend plan into consumable contracts -> unblocks frontend analytics work once the data layer is stable.

## Files to create or modify
- `packages/api/src/services/analytics.ts` (new)
- `packages/api/src/routes/analytics.ts` (new)
- `packages/api/src/index.ts`

## API contract
Implement these read-only routes using the shared schemas directly:
- `GET /v1/analytics/overview`
- `GET /v1/analytics/domains`
- `GET /v1/analytics/leaderboard/:domainId`
- `GET /v1/analytics/topic/:topicId`

## Shared types / schemas
- Import request/response parsing and typing from `@opndomain/shared`
- Do not create duplicate route-local DTOs for these contracts

## Database changes
- None in this task

## Frontend consumer
- Router analytics pages will consume these endpoints later
- Keep responses deterministic and presentation-ready, but do not add router code here

## Implementation requirements
- `overview` reads from `platform_daily_rollups` and computes cumulative totals in service code
- `domains` reads from `domain_daily_rollups` plus `domains`, defaulting to the latest available rollup date
- `leaderboard/:domainId` reads from `domain_reputation`, `beings`, and `domains`, ordered by `decayed_score DESC`
- `topic/:topicId` reads from `topics`, `rounds`, `contributions`, `contribution_scores`, and `claims`
- `topic/:topicId` must return `claimCount = 0` and `claimDensity = 0` when no claim rows exist
- Missing `domainId` or `topicId` should return the existing API-style not-found envelope

## Acceptance criteria
- All four endpoints exist under `/v1/analytics/*`
- Route parsing and response typing use shared contracts directly
- Query ordering is deterministic for stable frontend and test consumption

## Files that must not be modified
- `packages/router/**`
- `packages/mcp/**`
- authority docs
- unrelated API route families

title: Task 1: Define shared analytics contracts
description:
**Strategic context:**
This task -> lock the analytics API contracts in shared first -> prevents API/router drift in the approved [OPN-158](/OPN/issues/OPN-158#document-plan) backend rollout -> unblocks migration and route implementation against stable request/response shapes.

## Files to create or modify
- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/index.ts`
- optional new analytics contract file inside `packages/shared/src` only if it keeps the schemas clearer

## API contract
Define shared request/response schemas for:
- `GET /v1/analytics/overview`
  - query: `{ from?: YYYY-MM-DD, to?: YYYY-MM-DD }`
  - response: `{ generatedAt, window: { from, to }, totals: { totalTopics, totalContributions, totalVerdicts, activeBeings, activeAgents }, series: [{ rollupDate, topicsCreatedCount, contributionsCreatedCount, verdictsCreatedCount, cumulativeTopics, cumulativeContributions, cumulativeVerdicts, activeTopics, activeBeings, activeAgents }] }`

title: # Scale Plan v2: Single-DO Topics, Fast Flush, Incremental Publication
description:
make sure you consult the back end engineer and cto before starting this project and make sure you incorporste their feedback 

 \## Summary



  Keep the current Cloudflare shape, but optimize the real bottlenecks instead of redesigning ingest.



  \- Retain one Durable Object per topic as the hot write buffer and control plane.

  \- Do not add sharded ingest DOs or Cloudflare Queues for per-topic ingest.

  \- Prioritize:

      1\. observability and hard capacity targets

      2\. flush-path optimization

      3\. incremental transcript/state publication

      4\. async semantic scoring with graceful degradation

      5\. execution modes for autonomous vs scheduled\_event

      6\. D1 read scaling and lifecycle scaling



  The system should optimize for both:



  \- very large hotspot debates, up to 100k participants in one topic

  \- many concurrently active smaller topics



  autonomous debates target 30-60s public freshness. scheduled\_event debates get a premium execution profile with

  tighter freshness and stronger operational guarantees.



  \## Key Changes



  \### 1. Keep single-topic DOs, but make them authoritative for more hot-path state



  Use the existing per-topic DO as the acceptance buffer and topic control plane.



  \- Keep one DO per topic for:

      \- idempotency

      \- per-round contribution counts

      \- per-being pending vote counts / quotas

      \- round admission state

      \- hot topic counters used during live debate windows

  \- Move quota/accounting reads that are currently split across D1 + DO into the DO first, then materialize to D1

    later.

  \- Preserve D1 as canonical storage for finalized relational state, not hot-path coordination.



  \### 2. Optimize the flush path aggressively



  Replace the current tiny, synchronous, batch-followed-by-full-snapshot behavior.



  \- Raise D1 batch size after measurement instead of hard-coding the conservative 80 limit.

      \- Benchmark candidate sizes and adopt the largest stable batch size for contributions and votes.

  \- Flush contributions and votes independently and in parallel when safe.

  \- Remove “snapshot sync after every flush batch”.

      \- Debounce snapshot/state publication per topic.

      \- autonomous: publish at most once per 30-60s

      \- scheduled\_event: publish at a tighter debounce window during the live event

  \- Remove inline per-contribution vote recomputation from vote flush.

      \- Track affected contribution ids during flush

      \- recompute once per affected set after the vote batch drains

  \- Add drain modes:

      \- normal background drain

      \- accelerated drain for active premium events

      \- force-drain before scheduled result publication



  \### 3. Make semantic scoring asynchronous



  Do not block contribution acceptance on Workers AI.



  \- Accept contribution after auth, minimal eligibility, guardrails, and heuristic scoring.

  \- Persist an initial score payload immediately:

      \- heuristic score

      \- role detection

      \- guardrail output

      \- semantic status \= pending | degraded | complete

  \- Compute semantic scoring asynchronously and backfill later.

  \- Cache topic embedding and reusable topic-level semantic inputs per round.

  \- On Workers AI rate limit or timeout:

      \- accept contribution

      \- mark semantic scoring deferred

      \- backfill later without failing ingest

  \- scheduled\_event debates may run a tighter semantic backfill loop, but must still degrade gracefully.



  \### 4. Replace monolithic snapshot rebuilds with incremental publication



  This is the highest-priority read-path change.



  \- Stop rebuilding a full topic transcript from D1 on every flush.

  \- Publish transcript as incremental R2 artifacts:

      \- topics/{id}/rounds/{round}/pages/{page}.json

      \- append-only or replace-small-page behavior

  \- Publish a compact topic state object separately:

      \- counts

      \- round metadata

      \- transcript page index

      \- freshness timestamps

      \- verdict summary pointers

  \- Router topic pages should render from:

      \- compact topic state snapshot

      \- transcript page index

      \- transcript pages/chunks, not one giant transcript blob

  \- Add page/chunk sizing rules that make large debates bounded to render and cheap to cache.

  \- For scheduled\_event, maintain a “latest activity” or “live summary” object separate from the archival transcript.



  \### 5. Add execution modes and premium event behavior



  Make topic runtime policy explicit.



  \- Extend topic config with:

      \- executionMode \= autonomous | scheduled\_event

      \- serviceTier \= standard | premium

      \- eventSchedule for scheduled debates

  \- Behavior by mode:

      \- autonomous

          \- cheapest drain/publish profile

          \- 30-60s public freshness target

          \- no expensive live guarantees

      \- scheduled\_event

          \- prewarm coordinator DO before start

          \- tighter publish debounce during event

          \- accelerated flush/drain profile

          \- force-drain and canonicalize before publish deadline

          \- explicit admission control / participant cap / reserved capacity config

  \- Keep one codepath, but allow different operating parameters and protections by execution mode.



  \### 6. Improve D1 read scaling and lifecycle scaling



  Reduce avoidable primary pressure.



  \- Adopt D1 Sessions / read replication for router and other read-heavy paths.

  \- Keep large transcript reads off D1 entirely after incremental publication lands.

  \- Cache or materialize hot membership/quota inputs needed during debate windows.

  \- Replace broad lifecycle polling with narrower, event-aware scheduling where practical:

      \- keep cron as safety sweep

      \- add event-driven transitions for scheduled debates so thousands of topics do not depend on a single broad scan



  \## Public APIs / Interfaces / Types



  Add explicit runtime and publication contracts.



  \- Topic schema/config gains:

      \- executionMode

      \- serviceTier

      \- eventSchedule

      \- publication debounce / acceleration policy fields if needed internally

  \- Contribution response gains materialization semantics:

      \- accepted id

      \- score status (initial, semantic\_pending, complete)

      \- visibility / guardrail outcome

  \- Topic state/publication contract changes from “single transcript blob” to:

      \- compact topic state snapshot

      \- transcript page index

      \- transcript page/chunk payloads

  \- Internal types should explicitly model:

      \- pending semantic work

      \- affected-contribution recompute sets

      \- topic publication schedule / debounce state

      \- event drain / finalize mode



  \## Test Plan and Capacity Validation



  Ship the redesign with measured targets, not intuition.



  \- Add benchmark/load scenarios for:

      \- 100k participants in one topic over a bounded round window

      \- many active topics with moderate fan-in

      \- mixed load with one premium event plus background autonomous traffic

  \- Verify:

      \- contribution accept latency remains bounded while backlog grows

      \- snapshot publication frequency is bounded and no longer tied to every flush batch

      \- router renders very large debates from paged transcript artifacts without monolithic object fetches

      \- premium event finalize path drains and publishes on deadline

  \- Add observability before rollout:

      \- per-topic accept rate

      \- flush backlog depth

      \- drain throughput

      \- publication freshness lag

      \- semantic scoring backlog

      \- D1 overloaded / retry counts

      \- router cache hit rate and transcript page fetch cost



  \## Assumptions and Defaults



  \- Stay Cloudflare-only.

  \- Keep single DO per topic unless measurement later proves it is insufficient.

  \- Do not add Cloudflare Queues for per-topic ingest.

  \- Optimize for both mega-topics and many-topic concurrency.

  \- autonomous debates target 30-60s public freshness.

  \- scheduled\_event debates spend more for a smoother experience, but still rely on the same core architecture with

    tighter operating parameters.

  \- D1 remains canonical storage; the major changes are flush policy, publication model, and async scoring.

title: Data audit: scoring, reputation, epistemic, and engagement data completeness
description:
## Objective

Conduct a comprehensive audit of all data collected by the opndomain platform. Determine what we have, what we're missing, and what visualizations we should build to make the data compelling.

## Deliverables

1. **Data Inventory** � Catalog every data point collected across scoring (5-dim composite), reputation (domain decay, vote reliability, epistemic reliability), epistemic (claims, relations, resolutions), and engagement (rollups, topic lifecycle, participation).

2. **Gap Analysis** � Identify data we should be collecting but aren't. Consider: temporal trends, cross-domain signals, claim lifecycle metrics, contributor behavior patterns, scoring calibration signals.

3. **Visualization Recommendations** � For each major data surface, propose a visualization spec:
   - Claim graphs (network visualization of claim relations)
   - Knowledge graphs (domain-spanning claim networks)
   - Scoring distributions (per-topic, per-domain, per-being)
   - Reputation curves (decay over time, consistency vs. average)
   - Engagement dashboards (daily rollups, active beings, contribution velocity)

4. **Priority Ranking** � Rank all proposed additions and visualizations by: (a) impact on user engagement, (b) implementation complexity, (c) data availability.

## How to work

- Read the database migrations in `packages/api/src/db/`
- Read the scoring logic in `packages/api/src/lib/scoring/`
- Read the epistemic engine in `packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/`
- Read reputation services in `packages/api/src/services/reputation.ts`
- Read authority docs: `WHAT.md`, `docs/WORKING-TRUTH.md`, `docs/SCHEMA-CONTRACT.md`
- Put your analysis in an issue document with key `audit`
- Leave a comment summarizing key findings

## Success criteria

- Complete inventory of all collected data points
- At least 3 identified data gaps with proposed schema additions
- At least 5 visualization specs with data source, chart type, and interaction model
- Priority ranking that the CEO can use to dispatch engineering work

title: Redesign topic detail page: clean layout, featured response, claim graph
description:
## Objective

Redesign the /topics/:id page to fix the cluttered layout, surface the best response prominently, and get claim graphs working. Board feedback: the page has good data but poor presentation.

## Board Feedback (verbatim from OPN-142)

- Layout is cluttered
- No way to read full responses from each agent at each point
- Individual responses jammed together with no synthesis � reads awkwardly
- **The synthesized highest-scoring response should be published as THE answer**
- Prompt should be bold at the top, then the full response of the last round's highest scorer (with votes counting) as the answer
- Claim graphs and claims need to work
- Descriptive bubbles on the right are not liked
- Overall needs work

## Current State (from code audit)

The topic detail page currently has:
- Verdict summary section (closed topics)
- Two-column grid: overview card + score breakdown
- Narrative beats + highlights sections
- Claim graph section (exists but may not have data)
- Transcript: contributions shown by round, trimmed to 320 chars, no expand
- Share panel on closed topics

## Required Changes

### 1. Hero: Prompt as the Headline
- Topic prompt should be the bold, prominent headline at top
- Domain and metadata secondary

### 2. Featured Response (NEW � highest priority)
- After the prompt, show the **full response** of the highest-scoring contribution from the final round (factoring vote scores)
- This is THE answer � display it prominently, with the being handle and score as attribution
- No truncation � show the complete text

### 3. Clean Up Transcript
- Remove the 320-char truncation � let people read full contributions
- Better visual separation between contributions
- Group by round with clear round headers
- Consider collapsible rounds (expand/collapse)

### 4. Fix Claim Graph
- Verify claim graph data is being generated and passed to the page
- If data exists, ensure the visualization renders correctly
- If data is missing, this may need API/shared work (flag as separate task)

### 5. Remove Right-Side Descriptive Bubbles
- Board doesn't like them � remove or redesign

### 6. General Declutter
- Reduce visual noise
- Better typography hierarchy
- Mobile responsive

## Files
- `packages/router/src/index.ts` (topic handler, lines 506-613)
- `packages/router/src/lib/render.ts` (rendering functions)
- `packages/router/src/lib/tokens.ts` (TOPIC_DETAIL_PAGE_STYLES)

## Success Criteria
1. Topic prompt is the bold headline
2. Best response from final round displayed prominently as the answer
3. Full contributions readable (no 320-char truncation)
4. Claim graph renders when data exists
5. Right-side bubbles removed
6. Clean, uncluttered layout
7. Deploy and verify on the nuclear power topic

- Carousel of closed topics
- More information sections with little graphics as you scroll down
- Bottom nav bar

## Reference: paperclip.ing Layout (top to bottom)

1. **Top nav** � logo left, links right (Blog, Docs, GitHub)
2. **Hero** � full viewport, big headline, subtitle, two CTA buttons (Get Started + GitHub)
3. **Quickstart** � command snippet + brief setup explanation
4. **Social proof carousel** � testimonials/quotes (we adapt to closed topic verdicts carousel)

9. **Technical details** � under the hood

11. **Final CTA** � repeat quickstart
12. **Footer** � links organized by category

## Mapping to opndomain

|---|---|
| Hero + CTAs | Hero with "Connect via MCP" + "Get Started" |
| Quickstart | MCP/CLI/Plugin quickstart blurb |

description:

## Design Requirements
- Prompt-first hierarchy: the topic prompt is the dominant headline, with topic/domain/template/status metadata visually secondary.
- Featured answer: directly below the prompt, define a full-width module for the highest-scoring contribution from the final visible round, including being handle, round label, and final score.
- Transcript: specify readable round grouping, stronger contribution separation, full-response presentation without excerpt clipping, and a mobile-safe pattern for long blocks of text.
- Claim graph: specify placement and hierarchy when claim data exists and the empty/fallback treatment when claimGraph.available is false.
- Remove the current descriptive-bubble/right-rail feel. The page should read as one intentional editorial flow, not a dashboard with stray side widgets.

- At 640px and below, the featured answer, transcript blocks, and claim-graph section must collapse cleanly without causing cramped metadata stacks or unreadable long lines.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The spec is implementable entirely inside the current router topic page without new backend/shared/package work.
2. The prompt-first, featured-answer, transcript, and claim-graph sections are all defined clearly enough for direct implementation.
3. The spec references existing ender.ts and 	okens.ts patterns instead of proposing a new visual system.

title: Implement topic-format contract, lifecycle rules, snapshots, and MCP filtering
description:
**Strategic context:**
Build the authoritative topic-format contract and lifecycle support -> makes the approved two-format debate model real for [OPN-99](/OPN/issues/OPN-99) -> priority because the founder explicitly requested distinct scheduled and rolling participation modes in [OPN-98](/OPN/issues/OPN-98) -> unblocks MCP format-aware participation and later router presentation without contract drift.

## Objective

Implement the backend/shared/MCP side of the approved topic-format plan: persisted `topicFormat`, authoritative topic read/write contract, scheduled prompt-lock behavior, rolling quorum behavior, rolling successor creation, snapshot updates, and MCP format-aware discovery.

## Files To Create Or Modify

- `packages/shared/src/templates.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/index.ts`
- `packages/api/src/db/*.sql` for the required migration
- `packages/api/src/routes/topics.ts`
- `packages/api/src/routes/internal.ts`
- `packages/api/src/services/topics.ts`
- `packages/api/src/services/lifecycle.ts`
- `packages/api/src/lib/snapshot-sync.ts`
- `packages/api/src/index.ts` only if lifecycle cron wiring must change
- `packages/mcp/src/index.ts`
- tests in `packages/api/**`, `packages/mcp/**`, and shared schema tests if present

## API Contract

Add the shared contract in `packages/shared` and make `packages/api` authoritative for it.

### Shared schemas/types to add or extend

- `TopicFormatSchema` with exactly two protocol-safe enum values:
  - `scheduled_research`
  - `rolling_research`
- `TopicFormatSummarySchema` with fields the frontend/MCP will consume:
  - `label: string`
  - `joinWindow: "pre_start" | "rolling"`
  - `promptLock: boolean`
  - `quorumTarget: number | null`
  - `replenishes: boolean`
- Extend `CreateTopicSchema` with `topicFormat`
- Extend topic read DTOs returned by API services with:
  - `topicFormat`
  - `formatSummary`
  - `minDistinctParticipants`
  - `countdownSeconds`

### HTTP routes to update

- `POST /v1/topics`
  - Request body: existing fields plus `topicFormat`
  - `scheduled_research` may accept explicit `startsAt` and `joinUntil`; if omitted, apply controlled defaults in API
  - `rolling_research` uses `minDistinctParticipants` and `countdownSeconds`, defaulting quorum target to `5` when omitted
  - Response shape: existing topic payload plus `topicFormat`, `formatSummary`, `minDistinctParticipants`, `countdownSeconds`
- `GET /v1/topics`
  - Add optional `topicFormat` query filter
  - Every topic item returned includes `topicFormat`, `formatSummary`, `minDistinctParticipants`, `countdownSeconds`
- `GET /v1/topics/:topicId`
  - Include the same format fields
- `GET /v1/topics/:topicId/context`
  - Include the same format fields
- Prefer extending the existing `POST /v1/internal/topics/sweep` path for rolling successor creation. Only add a new internal route if reusing the current sweep path proves impossible.

## Database Changes

Add the minimal migration required to persist the format on `topics`.

Requirements:
- Add authoritative `topic_format` persistence on `topics`
- Reuse existing `topics.min_distinct_participants` and `topics.countdown_seconds`; do not add duplicate quorum config fields
- Do not introduce any `arena_*`, `channel_*`, or `message_*` identifiers

## Lifecycle / Behavior Requirements

### Scheduled Research
- Preserve fixed enrollment before start
- After the first non-creator participant joins, reject edits that would change topic prompt, start window, or join cutoff
- Keep the lock topic-level only; do not invent per-member config persistence in `topic_members`

### Rolling Research
- Persist and honor `minDistinctParticipants` / `countdownSeconds`
- Default quorum target to `5` when omitted for `rolling_research`
- Do not transition rolling topics to `stalled` just because `join_until` passes below quorum; they should remain joinable while waiting for quorum
- When a rolling topic leaves the joinable pool because it starts, create at most one successor open topic cloned from the same seed configuration if no open/countdown successor already exists
- Successor creation must stay in API-owned lifecycle logic with dedupe protection

## Snapshot / Cache Requirements

- Extend `packages/api/src/lib/snapshot-sync.ts` so `topics/{id}/state.json` includes `topicFormat` and `formatSummary`
- Ensure lifecycle transitions and successor creation still invalidate the relevant KV/cache keys so router topic and landing pages do not serve stale format state

## MCP Requirements

Update `packages/mcp/src/index.ts` so:
- `list-joinable-topics` accepts `topicFormat`
- `participate` accepts `topicFormat`
- MCP prefers authoritative API filtering rather than inferring from `templateId`
- MCP does not reimplement quorum or prompt-lock logic locally

## What The Frontend Will Consume

The later router task will consume:
- persisted `topicFormat` from topic rows for `/topics`
- `topicFormat` + `formatSummary` from API-owned snapshot state for `/topics/:topicId`

This task must make those fields authoritative and stable before frontend work begins.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `topicFormat` is persisted and returned consistently across create/list/get/context topic paths
- `TopicFormatSchema` and `TopicFormatSummarySchema` exist in `packages/shared` and are imported by API/MCP
- `minDistinctParticipants` and `countdownSeconds` are no longer phantom inputs; createTopic persists and read paths expose them
- Scheduled Research rejects prompt/start/join edits after external enrollment begins
- Rolling Research waits for quorum instead of stalling and creates at most one deduped successor topic through API-owned lifecycle logic
- `topics/{id}/state.json` includes `topicFormat` and `formatSummary`
- MCP can filter and participate by `topicFormat` without template-only inference
- Tests cover schema validation, create/list/get/context payloads, scheduled prompt-lock enforcement, rolling quorum behavior, successor deduplication, snapshot payload changes, and MCP filtering

## Files That Must Not Be Modified

- `packages/router/**`
- authority docs in repo root

## Notes

- Router work stays blocked until this contract is stable.
- Use protocol-language identifiers only. "Rolling Arena" may appear later as display copy, but not in code/schema/API names.

title: Design two debate formats: Scheduled Research and Rolling Arena
description:
## Objective

The founder wants two distinct participation formats for opndomain debates. Today we have a single flow (topic creation ? open ? countdown ? started). We need to formalize two user-facing debate formats that serve different purposes.

### Format 1: Scheduled Research Debates

- Topics with a fixed start time (e.g. 8am EST daily)
- Agents/participants queue up before the start time
- At the scheduled time, the debate begins with everyone who joined
- **Critical**: All participants must keep the same prompt/agent config for the entire debate � no hot-swapping mid-debate. This is for research consistency so we can compare contributions fairly.
- Join window closes at start time
- These produce higher-quality, controlled research artifacts

### Format 2: Rolling Arena (Always-On)

- System auto-creates debates on a ~60-second loop with random topics from a curated pool
- Pre-canned prompts or preloaded plugin prompts (Claude MCP, Codex, etc.) so new users can jump in instantly without writing a prompt
- **Quorum rule**: Topic runs when =5 agents are in the room. If <5, it waits and accumulates participants.
- Always a debate available for new users to join � this is the onboarding/demo experience
- Produces a continuous stream of research artifacts
- Designed for harness operators who want to set up a loop and let it run

## Context

- Current system has `scheduled`, `quorum`, and `rolling` cadence families in lifecycle.ts � these are building blocks but need to be composed into the two formats
- Topic templates exist (debate_v1, debate_v2, research, deep, socratic, chaos) � may need new templates or a format-level abstraction above templates
- The MCP participate tool already handles join ? wait ? contribute flow � needs to support both formats
- Parent issue: [OPN-98](/OPN/issues/OPN-98)

## Success Criteria

1. Plan covers data model changes (new fields, tables, or enums needed)
2. Plan covers topic auto-creation system for rolling arena (cron/worker, topic pool, prompt selection)
3. Plan covers prompt-locking mechanism for scheduled research mode
4. Plan covers quorum detection and auto-start logic for arena
5. Plan covers MCP and API changes needed for both formats
6. Plan is implementable in 1-2 sprints
7. Plan addresses how existing templates/cadence families map to or extend for these formats

title: Design new verdict page: architecture, data model, and interactive components
description:
## Objective

The current published verdict page is a bare data dump � raw text summary, truncated round excerpts, confidence badge. No visual design, no interactivity, no narrative flow. The founder has flagged this as "functionally useless."

We need a complete redesign of the verdict presentation layer. This is our shareable artifact � the thing that gets linked on social media, that brings new users, that demonstrates what opndomain does.

## What needs to happen

1. **Verdict statement redesign** � The summary should be a compelling, opinionated sentence that takes a clear position, not a bland meta-description. Consider generating a "headline verdict" distinct from the analytical summary.

2. **Visual score/confidence indicator** � A gauge, meter, radial chart, or some visual that immediately communicates the strength and confidence of the verdict. Not just text badges.

3. **Interactive argument map or claims graph** � We have epistemic claim extraction already built. Surface this as an interactive visualization � how arguments flowed, what claims were made, which were challenged, which survived. This is the "wow" factor.

4. **Round-by-round narrative** � Not truncated excerpts but a story arc: "The debate opened with X. Participant 2 challenged Y. By round 3, consensus emerged on Z." Consider generating this narrative at terminalization time.

5. **Key quotes/highlights** � Surface the 2-3 most impactful contribution excerpts with proper attribution and scores.

6. **Share-optimized OG cards** � The current OG image is a pixel-font bitmap. We need something that looks professional and eye-catching in social previews.

## Context

- Parent issue: [OPN-94](/OPN/issues/OPN-94)
- Current rendering: `packages/router/src/lib/render.ts` and `packages/api/src/services/artifacts.ts`
- Claim graph engine already exists: `packages/api/src/services/epistemic/`
- The CMO is separately working on what makes verdicts shareable from a marketing perspective � coordinate on messaging.

## Success criteria

- A plan that covers data model changes, new API endpoints or artifact generation, and front-end component architecture
- Interactive claims visualization approach (technology choice, data shape)
- Narrative generation approach (when it runs, what it produces)
- OG image redesign approach
- Plan should be implementable by the engineering team in 1-2 sprints

title: Task 1: add additive storage and shared contracts for transcript sequencing
description:
## Strategic context
This task -> Collective Inference scale architecture for vote-dominant scoring -> needed to make transcript reads and vote scaling safe at high-volume topics -> unblocks monotonic transcript deltas, dedicated transcript reads, and later adaptive scoring work.

## Objective
Add the additive storage, feature flags, DO contract types, and shared transcript schemas before any runtime behavior changes.

## Service / file scope
Modify only:
- `packages/api/src/db/009_adaptive_scoring.sql`
- `packages/api/src/lib/do/schema.ts`

- `packages/shared/src/env.ts`

- `packages/shared/src/index.ts`

## API / shared contract
Add shared contract ownership for the future authenticated transcript endpoint `GET /v1/topics/:topicId/transcript`.

Shared types/schemas to add or extend:
- transcript query schema with `since?: number`, `roundIndex?: number`, `limit?: number`, `cursor?: string`, `mode?: "full" | "summary"`
- transcript response DTO with `topicId`, `generatedAt`, `changeSequence`, `mode`, `page`, `delta`, and `rounds`
- delta metadata DTO for `available`, `fromSequence`, `toSequence`, and `checksum`
- scale-tier / adaptive scoring config constants as shared definitions only

## Database changes
Create additive migration `packages/api/src/db/009_adaptive_scoring.sql` with:
- `topics.change_sequence INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`

No destructive schema edits. No legacy naming.

## What later frontend/API consumers will use
This task does not ship a route by itself. It creates the single source of truth that later API work will use for transcript query validation and transcript response shapes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Feature flags for adaptive scoring, transcript deltas, and elastic rounds exist and default to `false`

title: Review Collective Inference Architecture plan: technical validation and security audit
description:
## Objective

The founder submitted a plan for vote-dominant scoring at scale (200K agents/round). CEO has done initial review. CTO must now validate the technical architecture, correct known issues, and assess security.

## Plan summary

7 changes across 4 phases:
1. **Change Sequence Versioning** � monotonic changeSequence counter replacing transcriptVersion for deltas
2. **Dedicated Transcript Endpoint** � GET /topics/:id/transcript with pagination, no breaking change
3. **Adaptive Vote Influence** � scale tiers (intimate?swarm) reducing semantic weight as distinctVoterCount grows
4. **Conditional Semantic Skip** � at swarm tier (>10K voters), skip embeddings entirely
5. **Elastic Round Timers** � duration scaled by participant count, persisted at activation
6. **Vote Recomputation Optimization** � precompute aggregates in DO, batch flush
7. **Transcript Serving Optimization** � summary mode, gzip, swarm cache headers

## Known issues to address

1. **Migration numbering**: Plan says `006_adaptive_scoring.sql` but migrations go up to `008_topic_formats.sql`. Must be `009_adaptive_scoring.sql`.
2. **File reference**: Plan says `WITHOUT_SEMANTICS_FALLBACK_WEIGHTS` is at composite.ts line 36-39, but it is defined in `packages/shared/src/weight-profiles.ts` lines 36-39 (composite.ts imports it).

## Security review required

1. **Anti-collusion at swarm tier**: Semantic scoring drops to 0.0 at >10K voters. The plan relies on trust-tier weighting + vote reliability + 5% heuristic floor. Is this sufficient? What sybil attack vectors remain?
2. **Delta protocol integrity**: Can changeSequence be manipulated? Is the R2 delta storage tamper-proof?
3. **Transcript endpoint**: Rate limiting, auth requirements, pagination abuse vectors
4. **DO state consistency**: activeParticipantCount computed from buffered + flushed counts � race conditions during concurrent writes?

## Success criteria

- Produce a revised plan document with corrected migration number and file refs
- Security assessment with risk ratings for each concern
- Confirm or revise the 4-phase rollout order
- Identify any missing test scenarios

Full plan text is in the parent issue [OPN-105](/OPN/issues/OPN-105) description.

title: Define verdict page messaging and shareability strategy
description:
## Objective

The published verdict page is the primary shareable artifact of opndomain � it is what gets linked on social media, embedded in newsletters, and shown to potential users. Right now it is a plain data dump that nobody would want to share.

Define what makes a verdict page compelling enough to share, and provide messaging guidance for the redesign.

## What we need from you

1. **Verdict headline formula** � How should we frame verdict conclusions? What tone, length, and structure makes someone want to click? Look at how Metacritic, Rotten Tomatoes, and Wirecutter frame their conclusions. What is our equivalent?

2. **Share copy templates** � Pre-written share text for X/Twitter, Reddit, and link previews. What framing gets engagement? "AI agents debated X and reached Y" vs "The verdict is in: Z" � test different angles.

3. **Visual identity for verdict cards** � What should the OG preview card communicate in 1 second? What visual metaphors work for "structured AI debate verdict"? Color, layout, information hierarchy.

4. **Call-to-action strategy** � When someone lands on a verdict page, what do we want them to do next? Join a debate? Browse more verdicts? Register an agent? Define the funnel.

5. **Competitive differentiation** � How do verdict pages position opndomain vs. ChatGPT conversations, Perplexity answers, or Reddit threads? What is uniquely valuable about a multi-agent structured verdict?

## Context

- Parent issue: [OPN-94](/OPN/issues/OPN-94)
- The CTO is separately designing the technical architecture � your output feeds into their design decisions
- Current verdict example is in the parent issue description

## Success criteria

- Concrete messaging framework with example copy
- Visual direction guidance (not designs, but principles)
- CTA recommendations ranked by priority
- Shareability playbook: what makes people share verdict links

title: Improve closed topic display and landing page verdict highlighting
description:
**Objective:** Make closed topics the centerpiece of the landing page and make individual closed topic pages clearer and more shareable.

**Landing page improvements:**
- Promote the "Recent verdicts" section higher on the page � these are our best content, they should be above the fold
- Show more than 3 verdicts (try 6 or a "View all" link)
- Each verdict card should show: title, confidence level, domain, and a 1-line summary excerpt
- Make verdict cards visually distinct and clickable

**Closed topic page improvements:**
- Add a clear verdict summary section at the top (before transcript) so readers immediately understand the outcome
- Structure the transcript for readability: clear round headers, contribution excerpts with scores, visual hierarchy
- Make the share panel more prominent � currently it's easy to miss
- Ensure the OG image preview looks good when shared on X/Reddit (test with social card validators)

**Design direction (from CEO, no CMO yet):**
- Verdicts are our proof that the protocol works � treat them as editorial content, not database rows
- A visitor should land on the homepage, see compelling verdict cards, click one, and immediately understand: what was debated, what was concluded, and want to share it
- Think newspaper front page: bold headlines, clear summaries, authoritative presentation

title: Backfill verdict artifacts for all closed topics
description:
**Objective:** Ensure every closed topic has published verdict cards (HTML + OG PNG).

**Steps:**
1. Query the live D1 database to find closed topics missing artifacts: `SELECT t.id, t.title FROM topics t WHERE t.status = 'closed' AND t.id NOT IN (SELECT topic_id FROM topic_artifacts WHERE artifact_status = 'published')`
2. For each topic missing artifacts, call `POST /v1/internal/topics/:topicId/reconcile-presentation` via the admin API
3. Verify all closed topics now have `artifact_status = 'published'` in `topic_artifacts`
4. Report which topics were backfilled and any that failed

**Context:** The artifact system generates verdict HTML and OG images on topic close, but topics that closed before the system was added or had errors may be missing artifacts. The admin endpoint already exists � this is a data backfill, not a code change.

title: Investigate and fix semantic scoring collapse in non-propose rounds
description:
## Problem

Two independent debate runs confirm the same pattern:

- **Propose round:** semantic scores ~87-88 (good)
- **All other rounds** (critique/refine/synthesize/predict): semantic scores collapse to ~33-36 (bad)

Data:
- [OPN-65](/OPN/issues/OPN-65): propose ~86.7-87.7, later rounds ~32.7-34.3
- [OPN-77](/OPN/issues/OPN-77): propose ~88.0, later rounds ~33.9-36.0

Heuristic scores stay stable (~48-50) across all rounds, confirming the issue is isolated to the semantic scorer.

## Impact

This means the composite/final scores are artificially depressed for 80% of contributions (4 of 5 rounds). It undermines the credibility of the scoring system and makes it impossible to differentiate quality in non-propose rounds.

## Investigation Direction

The semantic scorer likely:
1. Is calibrated primarily for initial position statements (novelty/topicality bias)
2. May not account for the different rhetorical structure of critique/refine/synthesize/predict rounds
3. May need round-aware prompting or scoring normalization

## Success Criteria

- Semantic scores for non-propose rounds reflect actual quality differences between contributions
- Score variance across rounds is reasonable (not compressed into a 3-point band)
- No regression in propose-round scoring quality
- Verified across at least 2 debate runs with different topics

title: Implement epistemic scoring system (claim graph + engine + reputation)
description:
## Objective

Build the epistemic scoring layer into opndomain. This is the claim graph, claim extraction, epistemic engine, and reputation feedback loop described in the authority docs and detailed in parent issue OPN-67.

## Why this matters

The scoring system is the core protocol differentiator. Without it, agent reputation is based only on heuristic/semantic contribution scores. The epistemic layer adds claim-level tracking, cross-topic graph linking, and verifiable resolution. This is what makes reputation earned and what produces the data-rich backend we can eventually commercialize.

## Scope

Parent issue OPN-67 contains a detailed 8-phase technical plan covering schema migration (9 tables), env flag, claim extraction, claim graph, epistemic engine, barrel exports, contribution wiring, and terminalization wiring. It also addresses 6 specific legacy bugs. Read the full description on OPN-67 before planning.

## Success criteria

- All 9 epistemic tables created and registered in migration system
- Claim extraction produces correct verifiability classifications (unit tests pass)
- Claims extracted and stored on contribution submission (when flag enabled)
- Epistemic engine runs at terminalization (fail-open, does not block topic closure)
- Reterminalization is idempotent (reputation not double-applied or zeroed)
- All integration tests from the plan pass
- Flag off = zero epistemic tables touched

## Context

- Legacy code at D:/moltzdev has working implementations to port from
- Authority docs: IDEAS-BANK.md, LLM_Claim_Extraction.txt, WORKING-TRUTH.md
- This is a selective port with rebuild-specific rewrites, not a verbatim copy
- Excludes LLM extraction path (heuristic only for now)

title: Scoring System
description:
build this scoring system into our platform and make sure you build it so it fits in the system and gives us the data rich backend that we can eventually sell. claim graph, any kind of data graph we can come up with. make sure you take notes as this project is large we dont want to see code drift



 Context



 The authority docs (IDEAS-BANK.md, LLM\_Claim\_Extraction.txt, WORKING-TRUTH.md) define a claim graph and epistemic

 engine as a "Credible Later-Layer System." The legacy codebase at D:/moltzdev has working implementations. The current

  rebuild has zero claim/epistemic infrastructure. This plan ports the deterministic pieces — heuristic claim

 extraction, claim graph linking, epistemic engine resolution, and reputation feedback — without the LLM extraction

 path.



 This is a selective port with rebuild-specific rewrites, not a verbatim copy. Six structural issues from the legacy

 code are addressed explicitly.



 \---

 Structural Issues Addressed



 1\. Reterminalization idempotency bug



 Problem: rebuildDomainReputation() wipes prior epistemic deltas, but claim\_reputation\_applied flags prevent

 reapplication.

 Fix: Add rebuildEpistemicAdjustmentsForTopic(db, topicId) — a deterministic function that recalculates all epistemic

 deltas from resolved claims and reapplies them fresh. Called on reterminalize path after rebuildDomainReputation().

 Ignores the claim\_reputation\_applied flag entirely — it resets all flags to 0, recalculates deltas from current

 resolution state, applies them, then sets flags to 1.



 2\. Prediction table dependencies throughout engine



 Problem: Legacy references being\_domain\_prediction\_reliability, being\_prediction\_reliability, and

 arena\_prediction\_scores in 3 functions, not just getPredictionSignal().

 Fix: Remove all three prediction queries entirely for this pass:

 \- getPredictionSignal() → returns null always (already handles gracefully)

 \- rebuildEpistemicReliabilityForBeingInDomain() lines 760-765 → predictiveReliability \= 50 (neutral default),

 predictionEvidenceConfidence \= 0

 \- computeTopicEpistemicEvaluation() lines 968-971 → prediction\_realization\_rate \= 50 (neutral default, no query)



 Each removal gets a // STUB: wire when prediction scoring is ported comment.



 3\. Round-kind assumptions in evaluation metrics



 Problem: Legacy hardcodes round\_type \= 'propose', 'critique', 'synthesize' which don't cover all rebuild round kinds

 (e.g., predict, refine, vote, default).

 Fix: Scope initial port to round-agnostic metrics only. Three metric rewrites:

 \- best\_initial\_claim\_survival → use sequence\_index \= 0 (first round) instead of round\_type \= 'propose' AND

 round\_number \= 1

 \- final\_synthesis\_claim\_survival → use the last completed round (MAX(sequence\_index) WHERE status \= 'completed')

 instead of round\_type \= 'synthesize'

 \- critique\_precision\_score → apply to any round where the contribution's detected role is 'critique' (from

 contribution\_scores.details\_json) rather than filtering on round\_kind \= 'critique'. For v1 simplification: apply to

 all contributions equally (score 50 default), with a // TODO: refine with role-aware critique detection comment.



 4\. Migration registration/generation plumbing



 Problem: Adding 006\_epistemic\_core.sql alone doesn't register it. The rebuild requires: schema.generated.ts

 regeneration, schema.ts import + API\_MIGRATIONS entry, and schema.test.ts assertions.

 Fix: Phase 1 includes all four files:

 \- Create 006\_epistemic\_core.sql

 \- Add EPISTEMIC\_CORE\_SQL entry to generate-schema-module.mjs

 \- Run generator to update schema.generated.ts

 \- Add import + migration entry in schema.ts

 \- Add test case in schema.test.ts



 5\. Claim flush failure blocking terminalization



 Problem: All unflushed pending\_aux rows (any table\_name) count toward remainingCount in flushPendingTopicState(). A

 claim flush failure or autoStakeClaimsForContribution() throw would block force-flush from draining, preventing topic

 closure.

 Fix: Two changes:

 \- Claim flush is idempotent: INSERT OR IGNORE into contribution\_claims, mark pending\_aux flushed immediately on D1

 write success. No throw on duplicate.

 \- Staking is decoupled: autoStakeClaimsForContribution() runs as best-effort after flush succeeds. Wrapped in

 try/catch — failure is logged but does not affect flushed status or remaining count. Staking can be retried later

 during terminalization (the epistemic engine orchestrator already calls stake settlement).



 6\. Epistemic engine as hard terminalization dependency



 Problem: If evaluateEpistemicEngineForTopic() throws, topic closure fails entirely. Too aggressive for a later-layer

 system.

 Fix: Epistemic evaluation runs fail-open behind the flag:

 if (env.ENABLE\_EPISTEMIC\_SCORING) {

   try {

     epistemicResult \= await evaluateEpistemicEngineForTopic(env.DB, topicId);

     await applyClaimResolutionToReputation(env.DB, topicId);

   } catch (error) {

     console.error(\`epistemic evaluation failed for topic ${topicId}, continuing terminalization\`, error);

   }

 }

 Verdict reasoning\_json includes epistemic metrics only when available. Terminalization never fails due to epistemics.



 \---

 Naming Translation (Legacy -> Rebuild)



 ┌─────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┐

 │               Legacy                │              Rebuild              │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ being\_channels / channel\_id         │ topics / topic\_id                 │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ being\_channel\_messages / message\_id │ contributions / contribution\_id   │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ sender\_being\_id                     │ being\_id (via contributions join) │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ arena\_contribution\_claims           │ contribution\_claims               │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ arena\_contribution\_epistemics       │ contribution\_epistemics           │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ arena\_topic\_epistemic\_evaluations   │ topic\_epistemic\_evaluations       │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ arena\_claim\_feedback                │ claim\_feedback                    │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ round\_type / round\_number           │ round\_kind / sequence\_index       │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤

 │ genId()                             │ createId(prefix)                  │

 └─────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┘



 \---

 Phase 1: Schema Migration + Registration



 Create packages/api/src/db/006\_epistemic\_core.sql



 9 tables ported from legacy migration-no-human-epistemics.sql with rebuild naming:



 1\. contribution\_claims — extracted claims per contribution. Key columns: id, contribution\_id, topic\_id, claim\_index,

 claim\_text, verifiability\_class, resolution\_status, resolution\_confidence, resolution\_method, epistemic\_weight,

 claim\_reputation\_applied (INTEGER 0/1), extraction\_backend DEFAULT 'heuristic-v2', matched\_cues\_json,

 classification\_reason, classification\_version, supersedes\_claim\_id, prediction\_target\_ref. UNIQUE(contribution\_id,

 claim\_index).

 2\. claim\_feedback — peer agree/disagree/uncertain. UNIQUE(claim\_id, responder\_being\_id, round\_id).

 3\. contribution\_epistemics — per-contribution epistemic breakdown (claim\_survival, predictive\_support,

 coherence\_support, independent\_convergence, critique\_precision, epistemic\_score, epistemic\_confidence).

 UNIQUE(contribution\_id).

 4\. claim\_resolution\_evidence — signal records (evidence\_type, signal\_value, signal\_confidence, payload\_json).

 5\. domain\_claim\_graph — cross-topic claim relationships (claim\_id, related\_claim\_id, relationship, confidence,

 source\_mechanism, domain\_id). UNIQUE(domain\_id, claim\_id, related\_claim\_id, relationship).

 6\. being\_epistemic\_reliability — per-being per-domain profile (counters + scores). UNIQUE(being\_id, domain\_id).

 7\. reputation\_stakes — claim outcome stakes. UNIQUE(claim\_id, being\_id).

 8\. claim\_challenges — direct challenges. UNIQUE(claim\_id, challenger\_being\_id).

 9\. topic\_epistemic\_evaluations — topic-level metrics. UNIQUE(topic\_id).



 Plus indexes and updated\_at triggers following 002\_phase2\_integrity.sql pattern.



 Modify packages/api/scripts/generate-schema-module.mjs

 Add entry: \["EPISTEMIC\_CORE\_SQL", "006\_epistemic\_core.sql"]



 Run node packages/api/scripts/generate-schema-module.mjs to regenerate schema.generated.ts.



 Modify packages/api/src/db/schema.ts

 \- Add import: EPISTEMIC\_CORE\_SQL

 \- Add to API\_MIGRATIONS: { tag: "006\_epistemic\_core", fileName: "006\_epistemic\_core.sql", sql: EPISTEMIC\_CORE\_SQL }



 Modify packages/api/src/db/schema.test.ts

 \- Add migration order assertion: include "006\_epistemic\_core" in tag list

 \- Add content assertions: contribution\_claims, domain\_claim\_graph, being\_epistemic\_reliability,

 claim\_reputation\_applied



 \---

 Phase 2: Env Flag



 Modify packages/shared/src/env.ts



 Add to BaseEnvSchema (follows ENABLE\_SEMANTIC\_SCORING pattern at line 30):

 ENABLE\_EPISTEMIC\_SCORING: z.union(\[z.boolean(), z.string()]).default(false).transform((v) \=> v \=\=\= true || v \=\=\=

 "true"),



 \---

 Phase 3: Claim Extraction (Pure Functions)



 Create packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/claim-extraction.ts



 Port of D:/moltzdev/packages/api/src/lib/claim-extraction.ts. Pure functions, zero DB access.



 Exports:

 \- type ClaimVerifiabilityClass — predictive | causal | operational | logical | normative | unfalsifiable

 \- interface StructuredClaim — text + verifiability\_class + classification\_reason + matched\_cues

 \- extractStructuredClaims(text, limit?) -> StructuredClaim\[]

 \- classifyClaim(value) -> { verifiability\_class, classification\_reason, matched\_cues }



 This file ports verbatim — all cue patterns, thresholds, and classification logic come directly from legacy.



 Create packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/claim-extraction.test.ts



 Unit tests exercising each verifiability class against known text snippets. Pure function tests, no DB.



 \---

 Phase 4: Claim Graph



 Create packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/claim-graph.ts



 Port of D:/moltzdev/packages/api/src/lib/claim-graph.ts.



 Pure functions (port verbatim): tokenize(), jaccardSimilarity(), classifyClaimRelationship(), pickCandidates(),

 repeatedPhraseScore()



 DB function (selective rewrite):

 \- updateDomainClaimGraphForTopic(db, topicId, domainId) — all SQL rewritten with rebuild table/column names

 \- Uses allRows() from lib/db.ts, createId("dcg") from lib/ids.ts

 \- D1 batch chunked to 100 statements max



 \---

 Phase 5: Epistemic Engine (Selective Port)



 Create packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/engine.ts



 Selective port of D:/moltzdev/packages/api/src/lib/epistemic-engine.ts with three categories of changes.



 A. SQL renames throughout



 All legacy table/column names -> rebuild names (see translation table above).



 B. Prediction dependencies removed (Issue #2)



 Three explicit removals:

 \- getPredictionSignal() → always returns null

 \- rebuildEpistemicReliabilityForBeingInDomain() → hardcoded predictiveReliability \= 50, predictionEvidenceConfidence \=

  0

 \- computeTopicEpistemicEvaluation() → hardcoded prediction\_realization\_rate \= 50



 Each gets // STUB: wire when prediction scoring is ported.



 C. Round-kind metrics made agnostic (Issue #3)



 \- best\_initial\_claim\_survival → WHERE r.sequence\_index \= 0 (first round, any kind)

 \- final\_synthesis\_claim\_survival → WHERE r.sequence\_index \= (SELECT MAX(sequence\_index) FROM rounds WHERE topic\_id \= ?

  AND status \= 'completed') (last completed round, any kind)

 \- critique\_precision\_score → default 50 for all contributions in this pass. // TODO: refine with role-aware critique

 detection



 D. Reterminalization-safe reputation (Issue #1)



 New function not in legacy:



 rebuildEpistemicAdjustmentsForTopic(db, topicId):

 1\. Reset claim\_reputation\_applied \= 0 for all claims in topic

 2\. For each resolved claim with confidence > 0: compute delta (supported +3, contradicted -5, weakened -2) \*

 confidence, capped \[-15, +12] per being per domain

 3\. Apply aggregated deltas via applyEpistemicAdjustment()

 4\. Set claim\_reputation\_applied \= 1



 This is the function called on reterminalize path. On normal terminalize, the existing

 applyClaimResolutionToReputation() (which processes only claim\_reputation\_applied \= 0 claims) is used.



 Exported functions



 \- evaluateEpistemicEngineForTopic(db, topicId) — master orchestrator

 \- resolveClaimsForTopic(db, topicId) — core resolution

 \- rebuildEpistemicReliabilityForTopic(db, topicId) — per-being reliability

 \- applyClaimResolutionToReputation(db, topicId) — incremental path (normal terminalize)

 \- rebuildEpistemicAdjustmentsForTopic(db, topicId) — full rebuild path (reterminalize)

 \- autoStakeClaimsForContribution(input) — auto-stake on machine-verifiable claims



 \---

 Phase 6: Barrel Export + Reputation Extension



 Create packages/api/src/lib/epistemic/index.ts — re-exports from all three modules.



 Modify packages/api/src/services/reputation.ts



 Add applyEpistemicAdjustment(env, domainId, beingId, delta):

 \- Reads current decayed\_score from domain\_reputation

 \- Adds capped delta, clamps to \[REPUTATION\_FLOOR, 100]

 \- Updates decayed\_score and last\_active\_at

 \- Single-row UPDATE, follows existing patterns in the file



 \---

 Phase 7: Wire Claim Extraction at Contribution Time



 Modify packages/api/src/routes/contributions.ts



 After scoreContribution() (line 279), before DO fetch (line 280):

 const claims \= c.env.ENABLE\_EPISTEMIC\_SCORING

   ? extractStructuredClaims(guardrail.bodyClean)

   : \[];

 Pass claims in DO payload.



 Modify packages/api/src/lib/do/schema.ts



 Add optional claims?: StructuredClaim\[] to TopicStateIngestRequest.



 Modify packages/api/src/lib/do/topic-state.ts



 In /contribute handler, after writing pending\_messages and pending\_scores: if payload.claims exists and is non-empty,

 insert each into pending\_aux with table\_name \= 'contribution\_claims', operation \= 'insert'.



 Modify packages/api/src/lib/do/flush.ts



 Add flushClaimRecords(state, env) (Issue #5 — idempotent + decoupled staking):

 1\. Read from pending\_aux WHERE table\_name \= 'contribution\_claims' AND flushed \= 0

 2\. Batch INSERT OR IGNORE into D1 contribution\_claims

 3\. Mark pending\_aux rows flushed immediately on D1 success

 4\. Best-effort staking (decoupled): wrap autoStakeClaimsForContribution() in try/catch per contribution. Failure

 logged, does not affect flush status or remaining count.



 Call from flushPendingTopicState() after flushContributionRecords() succeeds.



 \---

 Phase 8: Wire Epistemic Engine at Terminalization



 Modify packages/api/src/services/terminalization.ts



 In runTerminalizationSequence(), after reputation block (line 235), before evaluateTerminalizationMode (line 237):



 Fail-open (Issue #6):

 let epistemicResult \= null;

 if (env.ENABLE\_EPISTEMIC\_SCORING) {

   try {

     epistemicResult \= await evaluateEpistemicEngineForTopic(env.DB, topicId);

     if (options?.reterminalize) {

       await rebuildEpistemicAdjustmentsForTopic(env.DB, topicId);

     } else {

       await applyClaimResolutionToReputation(env.DB, topicId);

     }

   } catch (error) {

     console.error(\`epistemic evaluation failed for topic ${topicId}, continuing\`, error);

   }

 }



 Include epistemicResult in verdict reasoning\_json when non-null.



 \---

 File Summary

title: Run real debate topic end-to-end with substantive content and evaluate output
description:
## Objective

Run a complete debate topic through the live system (api.opndomain.com) using real, substantive content � not simulation boilerplate. We need to see what the protocol actually produces when agents debate a real question.

## Context

The existing simulation harness (`scripts/simulate-topic-lifecycle.mjs`) works but generates generic text like "Simulation note for...". We need to either:
1. Modify the harness to accept real contribution content, OR
2. Create a new script/approach that feeds real argumentative text through each round

## Topic

Use AI Safety domain (`dom_ai-safety`). Suggested topic:

> **Should AI systems be required to explain their reasoning before taking consequential actions?**

This is a good test topic because it has clear pro/con positions, allows for evidence, critique, synthesis, and prediction � matching the debate_v2 template rounds (propose ? critique ? refine ? synthesize ? predict).

## Requirements

1. Use the 3 existing test agents from `scripts/sim-agents.json`
2. Template: `debate_v2`, Domain: `dom_ai-safety`
3. Each participant should submit substantive, distinct content in each round:
   - **Propose:** Real positions with reasoning (150-300 words each)
   - **Critique:** Actual critiques of other positions
   - **Refine:** Updated positions incorporating critiques
   - **Synthesize:** Identify areas of agreement/disagreement
   - **Predict:** Forward-looking conclusions
4. Run against the live API (all 3 services are healthy)
5. After completion, retrieve and report:
   - All contribution scores (heuristic, semantic, composite)
   - The generated verdict/artifact (if any)
   - The full transcript
   - Any scoring anomalies or failures

## Admin Credentials

You will need the admin clientId/clientSecret to create the topic and drive sweeps. Check the deployed worker secrets or `.dev.vars` for `ADMIN_ALLOWED_CLIENT_IDS`.

## Success Criteria

- Topic runs to `closed` status with all 5 rounds completed
- Each participant has at least 1 contribution per round (15+ total contributions)
- Scoring pipeline runs on all contributions (no null/zero scores from failures)
- Verdict artifact is generated at terminalization
- Full report of scores and transcript is provided back to CEO for review

title: Backend: build topic lifecycle simulation runner
description:
## Objective
After [OPN-57](/OPN/issues/OPN-57) stabilizes the backend contract, finish the Node runner that drives a full topic lifecycle end to end through authoritative API routes only, using pre-created real agent credentials instead of any seeding route.
## Dependency
Start or resume this only after [OPN-57](/OPN/issues/OPN-57) is closed and its API/shared contract is stable.
## Scope
In scope:
- scripts/simulate-topic-lifecycle.mjs
- minimal inline usage/help text inside the script
- if needed, one very small adjacent note under scripts/ describing the config shape and invocation
Explicitly out:
- new package creation
- direct D1 reads or writes
- lifecycle logic duplication in the script
- router, MCP, CLI, or public auth contract changes
- any internal test-agent seeding route or test-mode env guard
## Required inputs
Support explicit flags or env for:
- API_BASE_URL
- ADMIN_CLIENT_ID
- ADMIN_CLIENT_SECRET
- either SIM_AGENT_CONFIG_PATH pointing to a JSON file or an equivalent inline JSON env var for participant credentials
- SIM_TEMPLATE_ID
- SIM_CADENCE_MINUTES
- SIM_DOMAIN_ID
Participant credential config should describe pre-created real agents, for example:
- [{ clientId, clientSecret, beingId? }]
The runner may create beings through the normal authenticated API only if a supplied participant credential does not already include one.
## Required flow
1. Mint an admin access token through POST /v1/auth/token.
2. Read participant credentials from config instead of calling any seeding endpoint.
3. Mint participant access tokens through the existing public token endpoint.
4. Ensure each participant has or creates a being through the normal authenticated API.
5. Create a topic through POST /v1/topics using cadenceOverrideMinutes.
6. Patch startsAt and joinUntil to a near-future window so the run completes in minutes.
7. Join every participant being before topic start.
8. Loop until terminal state:
- trigger POST /v1/internal/topics/sweep
- read GET /v1/topics/:topicId/context?beingId=...
- submit one contribution per being when that being has not yet contributed to the active contribution round
- submit votes only from the returned oteTargets
9. Print a structured summary with topic id, round transitions, contribution ids, vote ids, terminal status, and failures.
## Constraints
- Fail fast on unauthorized, forbidden, invalid-state, or contract-shape errors.
- Do not silently retry business-logic failures.
- The script is a client of the API. It must not reimplement lifecycle timing, vote-target selection, or D1 access.
- Use simulation-prefixed names/handles/titles so generated data is clearly identifiable.
- Keep good operator notes in either the script help or completion comment so we can feed the workflow back into harness/MCP instructions later.
## Verification and rollout note
Include in your completion comment:
- exact commands you ran, including pnpm --filter @opndomain/api test and the runner invocation
- the JSON config shape accepted by the script
- any protocol bugs surfaced by the first real run as follow-up items, not bundled fixes unless required to make the harness function
- cleanup steps for generated simulation topics/beings if you ran against a shared environment
## Acceptance criteria
- one command runs the full flow end to end against an environment where the CEO has already created and verified real participant agents
- the script uses only authoritative API routes and supplied credentials
- the script prints enough structured output to debug round progression and failures quickly
- no new package and no changes to router/MCP/CLI or public auth contracts
